item.title: Disputes & Differing Lead to Discord By Abu Idrees
item.itunes:author: Salafi Publications
item.itunes:summary:
[08/05/2026] Disputes & Differing Lead to Discord - Sh Abu Idrees Muhammad Khān حفظه الله. Khuṭbah at Masjid As-Sunnah An-Nabawiyyah, Aston, Birmingham, UK.
Some points discussed during this Khuṭbah:
– The greatest means that brings discord and weakness amongst the ummah is that there is disagreement between them. – {O you who believe! When you meet (an enemy) force, take a firm stand against them and remember the Name of Allah much so that you may be successful. And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute (with one another) lest you lose courage and your strength depart, and be patient. Surely, Allah is with those who are the Patient ones} [Surah al-Anfal (8): 45-46] – Disputes lead to failure. – Judge your disputes with the Qur’an, the Sunnah, and the way of the Salaf. – Satan loves discord amongst the Muslims, especially between the people of the Sunnah. – {O you who believe! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and those of you (Muslims) who are in authority. (And) if you differ in anything amongst yourselves, refer it to Allah and His Messenger, if you believe in Allah and in the Last Day. That is better and more suitable for final determination} [Surah al-Nisa (4): 59]. – Example of the story of the Battle of the Uhud and the archers. – The consequences of not uniting upon the truth and Allah’s Command. – Holding onto the Rope of Allah and not being divided is what Allah loves. – Have a good relationship with Allah, be united, and have patience.
item.itunes:subtitle: [08/05/2026] Disputes & Differing Lead to Discord…
item.description:
[08/05/2026] Disputes & Differing Lead to Discord - Sh Abu Idrees Muhammad Khān حفظه الله. Khuṭbah at Masjid As-Sunnah An-Nabawiyyah, Aston, Birmingham, UK.
Some points discussed during this Khuṭbah:
– The greatest means that brings discord and weakness amongst the ummah is that there is disagreement between them. – {O you who believe! When you meet (an enemy) force, take a firm stand against them and remember the Name of Allah much so that you may be successful. And obey Allah and His Messenger, and do not dispute (with one another) lest you lose courage and your strength depart, and be patient. Surely, Allah is with those who are the Patient ones} [Surah al-Anfal (8): 45-46] – Disputes lead to failure. – Judge your disputes with the Qur’an, the Sunnah, and the way of the Salaf. – Satan loves discord amongst the Muslims, especially between the people of the Sunnah. – {O you who believe! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and those of you (Muslims) who are in authority. (And) if you differ in anything amongst yourselves, refer it to Allah and His Messenger, if you believe in Allah and in the Last Day. That is better and more suitable for final determination} [Surah al-Nisa (4): 59]. – Example of the story of the Battle of the Uhud and the archers. – The consequences of not uniting upon the truth and Allah’s Command. – Holding onto the Rope of Allah and not being divided is what Allah loves. – Have a good relationship with Allah, be united, and have patience.

item.title: Al-Ikhlās - The Pure and Sound Intention By Abu Zayd Khalid
item.itunes:author: Salafi Publications
item.itunes:summary:
[08/05/2026] Al-Ikhlās - The Pure and Sound Intention - Sh Abu Zayd Khālid حفظه الله. Khuṭbah at Masjid Salafi, Small Heath, Birmingham, UK.
- The different types of intention. - Who are the deeds performed for? - The type of intention that requires great concern. - Are we intending Allāh with our actions? - Two people can perform the same action and receive different outcomes. - "Whosoever intends this worldly life…" - The final outcome. - The different levels of people due to their niyyah. - The true Hijrah. - Allāh does not accept except that which is purely for His sake. - Hiding one's deeds and keeping them a secret. - Announcing good deeds publicly.
item.itunes:subtitle: [08/05/2026] Al-Ikhlās - The Pure and Sound Inten…
item.description:
[08/05/2026] Al-Ikhlās - The Pure and Sound Intention - Sh Abu Zayd Khālid حفظه الله. Khuṭbah at Masjid Salafi, Small Heath, Birmingham, UK.
- The different types of intention. - Who are the deeds performed for? - The type of intention that requires great concern. - Are we intending Allāh with our actions? - Two people can perform the same action and receive different outcomes. - "Whosoever intends this worldly life…" - The final outcome. - The different levels of people due to their niyyah. - The true Hijrah. - Allāh does not accept except that which is purely for His sake. - Hiding one's deeds and keeping them a secret. - Announcing good deeds publicly.

item.title: Lesson 6 The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees
item.itunes:author: Salafi Publications
item.itunes:summary:
Lesson 6 The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees
– Shaykh Ibn al-‘Uthaymīn Q&A’s on Hajj – Ruling regarding wearing bright coloured clothes for women. – Is it better to perform the hajj on behalf of deceased parents, or fund it, or for the children performing hajj, or money given to charity? – The ruling on the one who commits the impermissible acts during Ihram. – The categories of the prohibitions and their related expiation: 1. Relations 2. Hunting 3. Marriage 4. Other matters – Losing excessive blood while in the state of Ihrām. – The ruling on fondling and that which leads to relations – The ruling of drinking Zamzam water – The Sunnah for the woman during sa’i when she approaches the green lights – Explanation regarding delaying the Tawaful Ifādah – Ruling regarding delaying the stoning on the Days of Tashrīq – Is the stoning just before noon acceptable? (Stoning on Day of Nahr).
item.itunes:subtitle: Lesson 6 The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees …
item.description:
Lesson 6 The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees
– Shaykh Ibn al-‘Uthaymīn Q&A’s on Hajj – Ruling regarding wearing bright coloured clothes for women. – Is it better to perform the hajj on behalf of deceased parents, or fund it, or for the children performing hajj, or money given to charity? – The ruling on the one who commits the impermissible acts during Ihram. – The categories of the prohibitions and their related expiation: 1. Relations 2. Hunting 3. Marriage 4. Other matters – Losing excessive blood while in the state of Ihrām. – The ruling on fondling and that which leads to relations – The ruling of drinking Zamzam water – The Sunnah for the woman during sa’i when she approaches the green lights – Explanation regarding delaying the Tawaful Ifādah – Ruling regarding delaying the stoning on the Days of Tashrīq – Is the stoning just before noon acceptable? (Stoning on Day of Nahr).

item.title: Lesson 5 The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees
item.itunes:author: Salafi Publications
item.itunes:summary: [Lesson 5] The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees. – What should a menstruating woman do during Hajj? – The woman who starts her menstruation after she has made her intention for Hajj, before Hajj starts. – The woman who starts her menses on the Days of Hajj. – Should a woman take medication to stop the menses? – The garments prohibited for the woman in the state of ihrām. – Pickin, etc.?g up the stones for the stoning of the Jamarāt. – Is it needed to sacrifice for Hajj? – What is upon a person who accidentally used scented soap etc. – Is it permissible to perform an umrah upon a deceased person? – Is it permissible to perform an umrah upon a living person? – Is it permissible to do repeat Umrahs in one trip? – Which prayers should be joined at Arafah? – Should men keep their right shoulder uncovered throughout? – The footwear for the men in the state of Ihrām. – Wearing footwear within the masjid and around the Ka’bah. – Should men carry an extra Ihrām to change into if it gets dirty? – Can the Hujjāj use toothpaste and brush their teeth? – What should one do if the organisers arrange transportation to leave for Mina too early?
item.itunes:subtitle: [Lesson 5] The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idre…
item.description: [Lesson 5] The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees. – What should a menstruating woman do during Hajj? – The woman who starts her menstruation after she has made her intention for Hajj, before Hajj starts. – The woman who starts her menses on the Days of Hajj. – Should a woman take medication to stop the menses? – The garments prohibited for the woman in the state of ihrām. – Pickin, etc.?g up the stones for the stoning of the Jamarāt. – Is it needed to sacrifice for Hajj? – What is upon a person who accidentally used scented soap etc. – Is it permissible to perform an umrah upon a deceased person? – Is it permissible to perform an umrah upon a living person? – Is it permissible to do repeat Umrahs in one trip? – Which prayers should be joined at Arafah? – Should men keep their right shoulder uncovered throughout? – The footwear for the men in the state of Ihrām. – Wearing footwear within the masjid and around the Ka’bah. – Should men carry an extra Ihrām to change into if it gets dirty? – Can the Hujjāj use toothpaste and brush their teeth? – What should one do if the organisers arrange transportation to leave for Mina too early?

item.title: Lesson 4 The Rights of Hajj By Shaykh Abu Idrees
item.itunes:author: Salafi Publications
item.itunes:summary:
– The stoning of the Jamarāt. – How and when the stoning of the Jamarāt takes place. – The correct time to carry out the stoning of the Jamarāt. – The days of Tashrīq - Stoning. – Stoning on behalf of someone else. – The specific times that one must remain in Mina. – Performing the farewell Tawāf. – A recap of the different types of Hajj: At-Tamattu’, Al-Qirān & Al-Ifrād. – The difference between a pillar of Hajj and an obligation of Hajj. – Missing a pillar of Hajj will invalidate one’s Hajj. – Missing an obligation of Hajj would not invalidate one’s Hajj. – Explaining the four pillars of Hajj. 1. The Intention 2. Standing at Arafah 3. Tawāf Al-Ifadah 4. As-Sa’ee – The various Obligations of Hajj. – The ruling on missing or leaving off an obligation of Hajj. – The sacrificial animal during Hajj. – Not being able to carry out the sacrifice. – The prohibitions of Ihrām - applicable to men and women. – The rulings of falling into that which is impermissible once in Ihrām.
– Questions: – A question regarding joining the prayers in the masjid. – Should one inform their relatives that they will be travelling to Hajj? – Can one use toothpaste, mints and chewing gum whilst in Ihrām? – Can a person remove the garment if Ihrām win hole in that state? – A question regarding a sister being on her menses and carrying out the Tawāf al-Ifādah. – Is it permissible to use moisturisers such as Vaseline when in the state of Ihrām?
item.itunes:subtitle: – The stoning of the Jamarāt. – How and when the …
item.description: – The stoning of the Jamarāt. – How and when the stoning of the Jamarāt takes place. – The correct time to carry out the stoning of the Jamarāt. – The days of Tashrīq - Stoning. – Stoning on behalf of someone else.
